stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2018 delivery. Forward enclave on Decks 16/18/19 with Concierge Area and Top Sail Lounge (Deck 16), MSC Yacht Club Restaurant (Deck 18), MSC Yacht Club Pool and Grill & Bar (Deck 19). Butler service and private access to MSC Aurea Spa section.

related.class_notes: Seaside Class. MSC Seaview is the second and final ship of the original Seaside class; her only direct sister is MSC Seaside (2017). The enlarged MSC Seashore (2021) and MSC Seascape (2022) form the separate Seaside EVO sub-class (170,412 GT on 339 m hulls), not direct sisters. Each Seaside-class sister uses different interior naming: Seaview has the Silver Dolphin + Golden Sand MDRs, the Odeon Theatre, and the Platinum Casino, while sister Seaside uses Ipanema + Seashore Restaurant, the Metropolitan Theater, and the Miami Casino. The Garage Club disco name is shared. The Asian Market Kitchen by Roy Yamaguchi chef collaboration (Pan-Asian trio) is class-wide and unique to the original Seaside pair — not on the Seaside EVO sisters.

deployment.winter_2025_26: 7-night South America cruises round-trip from Santos (São Paulo), Brazil, through April 2026.
deployment.current_ais_2026_04_11: VesselFinder reports MSC Seaview approaching Santos, Brazil, ETA 11 April 2026 ~09:15 local time, speed 19.1 kn — consistent with final Brazil-season rotations.
deployment.summer_2026: Mediterranean homeports (Barcelona, Marseille, Genoa, Naples, Palermo).
deployment.winter_2026_27: RETURNS to South America — Brazil and Argentina itineraries, per MSC announcement in April 2026 (originally slated for Southern Caribbean; rotation changed). Part of MSC's five-ship deployment in South America for 2026-27.

history.class_position: Second and final ship of the original Seaside class
history.covid_2020_2021: Laid up in Brazil for nearly a year during the 2020 pandemic pause; returned to Italy January 2021.
history.first_drydock: 31 March - 15 April 2023 at MSC's own facility in Valletta, Malta — scheduled maintenance, class works, hull cleaning and painting.
history.2026_01_12_fire_system_incident: 12 January 2026 — fire safety system water line burst on Deck 10 at ~07:45 during a South America cruise. Approximately 40 cabins flooded, guests displaced and relocated, MSC issued $150/cabin compensation and damage reimbursement. No injuries. Ship continued her itinerary.

discrepancies_flagged.imo_contamination: The previous page had IMO 9745366 — which is actually MSC SEASIDE's IMO. This was the seventh IMO cross-contamination caught across the MSC fleet repair (after Bellissima, Euribia, Magnifica, Meraviglia, Musica/Orchestra swap, and the Seascape-vs-tug-LUBRANI mix-up). Correct IMO for MSC Seaview is 9745378.
discrepancies_flagged.length: Fincantieri/MSC marketing spec gives 323 m LOA; VesselFinder's registry-side value shows 296.19 m. All other sources (Fincantieri, CruiseMapper, Wikipedia, etc.) agree on 323 m — the 296 m figure is likely a registry bow-exclusion or LBP calculation, not LOA. Use 323 m.
discrepancies_flagged.mmsi_outlier: A single stray search result mentioned '229125000' for MSC Seaview — incorrect. Confirmed value across 5+ sources is 248717000.
discrepancies_flagged.sisters: Seaview's only direct sister is MSC Seaside. Seashore and Seascape are the separate Seaside EVO sub-class, not direct sisters.
discrepancies_flagged.interior_naming: Each Seaside-class sister uses different MDR, theatre, and casino names. Do not copy Seaside's names onto Seaview. Verified from Seaview's own ship map PDF.
